key_insight: Widely used DFT simulations of liquid water with revPBE-D3 are not fully reproducible across codes because numerical setup and sampling choices can shift predicted density and diffusion significantly.
engineering_relevance: The study establishes consensus benchmark values for liquid water, giving developers a reliable reference for validating and comparing DFT-based ab initio molecular dynamics implementations.
method: Used long-range machine-learning interatomic potentials to enable robust statistical sampling of liquid water.
Trained and benchmarked against carefully converged DFT reference data.
Cross-checked results across six diverse community codes to isolate basis-set and pseudopotential effects.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports discrepancies of over 20% in diffusion coefficient and 10% in density in prior revPBE-D3 studies, and the new workflow resolves these differences across six codes.
watch_item: Benchmark agreement depends on careful convergence of basis sets, pseudopotentials, and sampling; weaker implementations can still bias density and diffusion estimates.
paper_title: How reproducible are first-principles simulations of liquid water?
